steps.title: Identify like terms
steps.content: The expression \(20x^2 + 3 + 5x^2\) has two terms with \(x^2\): \(20x^2\) and \(5x^2\). The constant 3 has no like term.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Add the \(x^2\) coefficients:
$$20x^2 + 5x^2 = 25x^2$$
The simplified expression is \(25x^2 + 3\).

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: \(25x^4 + 3\) — the exponent 4 is wrong; combining \(x^2\) terms does not change the exponent. Incorrect.
  • B: \(25x^2 + 3\) — matches our result exactly. That matches choice B — B is correct.
  • C: \(28x^4\) — the coefficient 28 and exponent 4 are both wrong, and the constant 3 is missing. Incorrect.
  • D: \(28x^2\) — the coefficient should be 25, not 28 (\(20 + 5 = 25\), not 28), and the constant 3 is missing. Incorrect.
